_meta.purpose: Worked raw->formatted examples. Include 1-2 examples for the relevant modality in each prompt. These teach house style and the corrections/flags behavior far better than instructions alone. REPLACE the 'output' side of these with your own centre's actual finalized reports once you have them — your radiologist's real wording is the best teacher. Keep examples de-identified.
_meta.how_to_use: For a given request, select examples whose modality matches. 1 matching example is usually enough with Gemini; 2 for trickier modalities. Put them after the system prompt and before the live raw_text.

input.raw_text: liver normal size echotexure no focal lesion ihbr not dilated. gb shows a 8 mm calculus, wall normal. cbd normal. pancreas normal. spleen normal. rt kidney 9.2 cm normal, no calculas no hydronephrosis. lt kidney mild hydronephrosis with a 6mm calculas at pelvis. no free fluid

output.formatted_report:
TECHNIQUE:
Real-time grey-scale ultrasonography of the abdomen was performed using a curvilinear transducer.

FINDINGS:
Liver is normal in size and echotexture. No focal lesion. Intrahepatic biliary radicles (IHBR) are not dilated.
Gallbladder shows an 8 mm calculus. Wall thickness is normal.
Common bile duct (CBD) is normal in caliber.
Pancreas is normal in size and echotexture.
Spleen is normal in size and echotexture.
Right kidney measures 9.2 cm, normal in echotexture. No calculus or hydronephrosis.
Left kidney shows mild hydronephrosis with a 6 mm calculus at the renal pelvis.
No free fluid in the abdomen.

IMPRESSION:
1. Cholelithiasis (8 mm gallbladder calculus).
2. Left renal pelvic calculus (6 mm) with mild left hydronephrosis.

sections.TECHNIQUE: Real-time grey-scale ultrasonography of the abdomen was performed using a curvilinear transducer.
sections.FINDINGS:
Liver is normal in size and echotexture. No focal lesion. Intrahepatic biliary radicles (IHBR) are not dilated.
Gallbladder shows an 8 mm calculus. Wall thickness is normal.
Common bile duct (CBD) is normal in caliber.
Pancreas is normal in size and echotexture.
Spleen is normal in size and echotexture.
Right kidney measures 9.2 cm, normal in echotexture. No calculus or hydronephrosis.
Left kidney shows mild hydronephrosis with a 6 mm calculus at the renal pelvis.
No free fluid in the abdomen.
sections.IMPRESSION:
1. Cholelithiasis (8 mm gallbladder calculus).
2. Left renal pelvic calculus (6 mm) with mild left hydronephrosis.

unchanged_protected: negations 'no calculus', 'no hydronephrosis', 'no free fluid' preserved

input.raw_text: lungs clear no consolidaton. there is mild cardiomegaly. cp angles clear. no effussion. bones nad

output.formatted_report:
TECHNIQUE:
Frontal (PA) view of the chest.

FINDINGS:
Both lung fields are clear. No focal consolidation, mass, or cavitation.
No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
Mild cardiomegaly. Mediastinal contours are normal.
Both hila are normal in size, density, and position.
The visualized bony thoracic cage is normal.
Both domes of the diaphragm are normal. Costophrenic angles are clear.

IMPRESSION:
1. Mild cardiomegaly.
2. No active lung parenchymal lesion.

sections.FINDINGS:
Both lung fields are clear. No focal consolidation, mass, or cavitation.
No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.
Mild cardiomegaly. Mediastinal contours are normal.
Both hila are normal in size, density, and position.
The visualized bony thoracic cage is normal.
Both domes of the diaphragm are normal. Costophrenic angles are clear.
sections.IMPRESSION:
1. Mild cardiomegaly.
2. No active lung parenchymal lesion.

corrections.to: The visualized bony thoracic cage is normal.

flags.issue: Cardiomegaly cannot be definitively assessed on a PA chest film without cardiothoracic ratio; radiologist to confirm the degree.

input.raw_text: ncct brain. small hyperdense focus rt basal ganglia ~ 12 x 9 mm s/o acute hemorage. mild perilesional edema. no midline shift. ventricles normal. rest parenchyma normal.

output.formatted_report:
TECHNIQUE:
Axial non-contrast CT (NCCT) sections of the brain were obtained from the skull base to the vertex.

FINDINGS:
A small hyperdense focus is noted in the right basal ganglia, measuring approximately 12 x 9 mm, suggestive of an acute hemorrhage. Mild perilesional edema is present.
No midline shift.
Ventricular system is normal in size and configuration.
The rest of the cerebral and cerebellar parenchyma shows normal attenuation. No other focal lesion.
Grey-white matter differentiation is preserved.
Visualized calvarium and skull base are normal.

IMPRESSION:
1. Small acute hemorrhage (approximately 12 x 9 mm) in the right basal ganglia with mild perilesional edema.
2. No mass effect or midline shift.

sections.TECHNIQUE: Axial non-contrast CT (NCCT) sections of the brain were obtained from the skull base to the vertex.
sections.FINDINGS:
A small hyperdense focus is noted in the right basal ganglia, measuring approximately 12 x 9 mm, suggestive of an acute hemorrhage. Mild perilesional edema is present.
No midline shift.
Ventricular system is normal in size and configuration.
The rest of the cerebral and cerebellar parenchyma shows normal attenuation. No other focal lesion.
Grey-white matter differentiation is preserved.
Visualized calvarium and skull base are normal.
sections.IMPRESSION:
1. Small acute hemorrhage (approximately 12 x 9 mm) in the right basal ganglia with mild perilesional edema.
2. No mass effect or midline shift.

input.raw_text: lumbar lordosis normal. l4-l5 diffuse disc bulge with mild thecal sac indentation and bilateral neural foraminal narowing. l5-s1 posterocentral protruison indenting the thecal sac, no significant canal stenosis. other levels normal. conus normal.

output.formatted_report:
TECHNIQUE:
Multiplanar T1W, T2W, and STIR sequences of the lumbosacral spine were obtained in sagittal and axial planes.

FINDINGS:
Normal lumbar lordosis. No listhesis.
At L4-L5, there is a diffuse disc bulge causing mild indentation of the thecal sac, with bilateral neural foraminal narrowing.
At L5-S1, there is a posterocentral disc protrusion indenting the thecal sac. No significant canal stenosis.
The remaining intervertebral disc levels are normal.
Visualized spinal cord and conus medullaris are normal in signal, with the conus terminating at the normal level.

IMPRESSION:
1. L4-L5 diffuse disc bulge with mild thecal sac indentation and bilateral neural foraminal narrowing.
2. L5-S1 posterocentral disc protrusion indenting the thecal sac, without significant canal stenosis.

sections.TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar T1W, T2W, and STIR sequences of the lumbosacral spine were obtained in sagittal and axial planes.
sections.FINDINGS:
Normal lumbar lordosis. No listhesis.
At L4-L5, there is a diffuse disc bulge causing mild indentation of the thecal sac, with bilateral neural foraminal narrowing.
At L5-S1, there is a posterocentral disc protrusion indenting the thecal sac. No significant canal stenosis.
The remaining intervertebral disc levels are normal.
Visualized spinal cord and conus medullaris are normal in signal, with the conus terminating at the normal level.
sections.IMPRESSION:
1. L4-L5 diffuse disc bulge with mild thecal sac indentation and bilateral neural foraminal narrowing.
2. L5-S1 posterocentral disc protrusion indenting the thecal sac, without significant canal stenosis.

unchanged_protected: Vertebral levels L4-L5 and L5-S1 preserved exactly
